title: Tangy Uruguayan Fish Terrine
description: A light, tangy fish terrine perfect for weeknight dinners, seasoned with fajita spices and set in a bain‑maríe to keep it silky and low‑calorie.
instructions: In a medium bowl, combine the cod fillets, fajita seasoning, low‑fat Greek yogurt, lime juice, chopped cilantro, sliced onion, dried oregano, salt, and pepper until well coated.
Whisk in the low‑fat milk until the mixture is smooth and the fish is evenly distributed.
Spoon the fish custard into individual mini cocottes, filling each about two‑thirds full.
Arrange the mini cocottes in a shallow baking dish and pour hot water into the dish until it reaches halfway up the sides of the cocottes.
Cover the baking dish with foil and bake in a preheated oven at 350°F until the fish terrine reaches 145°F, about 25 minutes.
Remove the dish from the oven, let the terrines cool for 5 minutes, then serve straight from the cocottes with a sprinkle of fresh parsley and a wedge of lime.
